content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 · SACRAMENTO, 95804 · · · 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

June 22, 1977

Honorable Paul Priolo
The State Assembly
State Capitol
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Opinion Request No. 77-017

Dear Mr. Priolo:

Thank you for your letter of May 25, 1977, requesting an opinion with regard to contributions received by the Republican National Committee. Because your opinion request does not raise a substantial question of interpretation under the Political Reform Act, no opinion will be issued. I hope, however, that the following informal advice answers your question.

You asked which contributors must be disclosed by the Republican National Committee if the Committee spends 10 percent of its funds in California. As you know, the Political Reform Act regulates candidates for state and local office, not federal office. Consequently, this response assumes that the 10 percent figure used in your letter refers only to state and local candidates. If the Committee spends 10 percent of its funds supporting such candidates, the Committee must disclose the names of contributors who give $500 or more to the Committee. This is because the pro rata share of the contribution attributable to California will be $50 or more, the threshold for disclosure pursuant to Government Code Section 84210(g). Of course, if a contributor earmarks or specifically designates $50 or more as a contribution to California candidates, that contributor must be disclosed.

Once the Committee has collected $500 or more in contributions to California state and local candidates, it will be a committee within the meaning of Section 82010(g). For ease of calculation, you may assume that $500 has not been collected for use in connection with California elections until at least $500 has been disbursed in connection with such elections. As such, it will be required to file a Statement

28

TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE SERVICES

Honorable Paul Priolo
Page two
June 22, 1977


of Organization pursuant to Sections 84100, et seq. and cam-
paign statements pursuant to Sections 84200, et seq.

If you wish to appeal the denial of your opinion
request you may do so pursuant to 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section
18321. If you have any further questions, please contact
Natalie West, an attorney with the Commission's Legal Division.

Sincerely,

[signature]

Michael Bennett
Executive Director

STATE CAPITOL
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
TEL: (916) 445-8635

J
4883 TOPANGA CANYON BLVD.
WOODLAND HILLS, CALIFORNIA 91364
TEL: (213) 883-0400
(SCS) 467-4134

77 017

Date Received 5-25-77
Response Due 6-8-777

Assembly
California Legislature

PAUL PRIOLO
ASSEMBLYMAN, THIRTY-EIGHTH DISTRICT

MINORITY LEADER

May 25, 1977


Daniel H. Lowenstein, Chairman
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Dan:

     I would like to have an opinion from the Fair
Political Practices Commission on the following situation.

     If the Republican National Committee planned to
expend 10% of its money in California, at what level would
the Committee be required to disclose contributors to meet
the requirements of California disclosure laws.

     Please do not hesitate to call me if you should need
additional information to render an opinion.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        PAUL PRIOLO
